Content: Schafer: Mmm, my new notebook.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，我的新筆記本。

Content: did you notice I brought my old and my new notebooks?
Content_zh: 你有注意到我帶了舊筆記本和新筆記本嗎？

Content: Can you believe I filled this up?
Content_zh: 你敢相信我把這本寫滿了嗎？

Content: Remember? This old notebook.
Content_zh: 記得嗎？這本舊筆記本。

Content: It's all full.
Content_zh: 全部都寫滿了。

Content: Now there's the design for the last shot in the game.
Content_zh: 現在裡面有遊戲最後一幕的設計。

Content: Can't show you, you guys always use those.
Content_zh: 不能給你們看，你們老是會拿那些畫面去用。

Content: So yeah, this is the new notebook.
Content_zh: 所以對，這是新的筆記本。

Content: And this is kind of a disaster first, because this, I didn't notice until I started it, it's wide ruled, instead of college rule.
Content_zh: 一開始有點災難，因為我開始用才發現，這是寬行距，不是大學行距。

Content: And this game is definitely college level.
Content_zh: 而這款遊戲絕對是大學程度的。

Content: But I decided actually this will make me feel like we got a lot more work done, 'cause my free writing will fill up more pages.
Content_zh: 不過我後來覺得這樣其實會讓我感覺我們做了更多事，因為我隨手寫寫就會寫滿更多頁。

Content: Yeah, I purposely got white so it'd feel pristine and fresh.
Content_zh: 對，我特地買白色，這樣感覺很乾淨、很新。

Content: It's smaller This is like totally like a baby notebook.
Content_zh: 它比較小，這根本像嬰兒筆記本。

Content: It does! It makes me look stupid, doesn't it?
Content_zh: 真的！它讓我看起來很蠢，對吧？

Content: But then again, I'm getting older so it's hard to see stuff.
Content_zh: 但話說回來，我年紀大了，東西也越來越看不清楚。

Content: New notebook.
Content_zh: 新筆記本。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Yeah. Let's talk about stuff.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：對，來聊點事情吧。

Content: We shipped Red's last week.
Content_zh: 我們上週推出了 Red’s。

Content: I don't know if you guys noticed that, we shipped a game.
Content_zh: 不知道你們有沒有注意到，我們推出了一款遊戲。

Content: It was awesome.
Content_zh: 超棒的。

Content: Yay, Red's.
Content_zh: 耶，Red’s。

Content: [Applause] It has, it has, it has made about $800,000 so far on the old Steam thing,
Content_zh: [掌聲] 到目前為止，它在老 Steam 上大概賺了 80 萬美元，

Content: which is fun.
Content_zh: 這還挺有趣的。

Content: And that's not a big enough number that we're going to be immediately completely independent of publishers this year,
Content_zh: 但這個數字還沒有大到讓我們今年馬上完全不靠發行商，

Content: like was a secret, back of my head kind of hope.
Content_zh: 雖然我心底偷偷有那麼一點希望。

Content: Where all my hopes are in the back of my head.
Content_zh: 我的所有希望都藏在腦後。

Content: It's a little, it's a little tricky financially because, pretty much, you know, I think conservatively we would estimate that
Content_zh: 財務上有點、有點棘手，因為基本上，我想保守估計的話，

Content: we've made most of the money that we're going to make off of it.
Content_zh: 我們已經賺到它大部分能賺的錢了。

Content: We might make more when we release Part Two, but it's not going to be equal.
Content_zh: 等我們推出第二幕時可能還會再賺一些，但不會一樣多。

Content: It's not going to be like the equal amount of sales.
Content_zh: 不會是同樣等級的銷量。

Content: So we're working on something that is, we're usually working on something like, oh, when this, when we release it, it'll make a bunch of money for us
Content_zh: 所以我們現在做的東西，通常會是那種，喔，等我們推出後，它會替我們賺一大筆錢，

Content: and that'll pay for more stuff and so we already got, or kind of got that money in advance twice.
Content_zh: 那筆錢可以拿來做更多東西；可是這次那筆錢我們已經算是提前拿過兩次了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: So you're making a bigger, more complex game that you know isn't going to sell as well?
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以你們正在做一款更大、更複雜，而且明知道賣得不會一樣好的遊戲？

Content: Schafer: Yeah. Yep. Just from love.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。沒錯。純粹出於愛。

Content: This time it's just for love.
Content_zh: 這次真的只是為了愛。

Content: That's our slogan.
Content_zh: 那就是我們的標語。

Content: The money is gone, now we're just running on love.
Content_zh: 錢沒了，現在我們只靠愛在撐。

Content: No, I mean, the first part made enough money.
Content_zh: 不是啦，我是說，第一幕賺的錢夠了。

Content: It definitely made its numbers.
Content_zh: 它確實達標了。

Content: I mean, it was, we went through these phases of like let's, let's estimate, you know, conservatively, because it's an adventure game
Content_zh: 我是說，我們經歷過幾個階段，像是，來吧，我們保守估計一下，因為這是冒險遊戲，

Content: and you know, we had a conservative estimate for it, how much it would sell.
Content_zh: 所以我們對它會賣多少有一個保守估計。

Content: Schafer: Everyone think in your head, how many units this game is going to sell.
Content_zh: Schafer：大家在心裡想一下，這款遊戲會賣出多少套。

Content: How many do you think it's going to sell?
Content_zh: 你覺得會賣多少？

Content: Rice: A million.
Content_zh: Rice：一百萬。

Content: Schafer: A million?
Content_zh: Schafer：一百萬？

Content: How many you think's going to sell?
Content_zh: 你覺得會賣多少？

Content: Bailey: 150,000 year one.
Content_zh: Bailey：第一年十五萬。

Content: Schafer: What do you say?
Content_zh: Schafer：你覺得呢？

Content: Stamos: I was going to say 200, but I was thinking more lifetime.
Content_zh: Stamos：我本來想說二十萬，但我想的是整個生命週期。

Content: Schafer: So let's say it was 300.
Content_zh: Schafer：那就先假設是三十萬吧。

Content: And then somewhere along the line we got really excited about people's response to it and excited by the game itself and how much we liked it.
Content_zh: 然後到某個時候，我們對大家的反應變得很興奮，也對遊戲本身和我們有多喜歡它感到興奮。

Content: And you can see this, you know, part of us were like, hey now, if this does really, really well, it might change a lot of stuff for us.
Content_zh: 你們也看得出來，我們有一部分會想，欸，如果它真的賣得非常非常好，可能會替我們改變很多事。

Content: Stoddard: A lot of people here kind of hang on because like, they know, they know Tim, they know, they know the management,
Content_zh: Stoddard：這裡很多人會撐著，是因為他們認識 Tim，認識管理層，

Content: they know the vision and if it hits, it'll, it'll be really good.
Content_zh: 也知道那個願景。如果成功了，就會真的很棒。

Content: It'll be really good.
Content_zh: 真的會很棒。

Content: So we're just hanging in.
Content_zh: 所以我們就繼續撐著。

Content: Schafer: And you could see that in like Camden.
Content_zh: Schafer：你在 Camden 那邊也看得到這點。

Content: I think Camden was like a lot of people were, kind of hoping for it.
Content_zh: 我覺得 Camden 當時很多人都有點在期待這件事。

Content: And it didn't sell that much.
Content_zh: 但它沒有賣那麼多。

Content: It didn't sell so much that it changed our whole business plan for the year.
Content_zh: 沒有賣到足以改變我們今年整個商業計畫。

Content: It sold what we kind of estimated originally.
Content_zh: 它賣得大概就是我們一開始估的數字。

Content: Schafer: So far it's made about 1.6 million dollars on Steam, which is awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：到目前為止，它在 Steam 上大概賺了 160 萬美元，這很棒。

Content: And it's, by, that's about 70,000 people purchased it; about 80,000 were the backers.
Content_zh: 其中大概有 7 萬人購買；另外大概 8 萬人是贊助者。

Content: So that's about 150,000 units out there.
Content_zh: 所以市面上大概有 15 萬套。

Content: Schafer: And so that's, I don't want be too sad about it [inaudible 00:04:41].
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我也不想太難過，[聽不清 00:04:41]。

Content: Just a little part of me was like, what if, you know, it's always part of you that hopes what if something really,
Content_zh: 只是我心裡有一小部分會想，如果呢？你知道，人總會有一部分希望，萬一真的有什麼，

Content: like a big, you know, outlier thing is just crazy, hits.
Content_zh: 像是很大的、很誇張的意外爆紅發生呢？

Content: It just unexpectedly, like our Kickstarter.
Content_zh: 就像我們的 Kickstarter 那樣突然發生。

Content: Once you have an experience like that, it's hard to go back to the normal world where things sell what you expect them to.
Content_zh: 一旦有過那種經驗，就很難回到正常世界，接受東西只會賣到你預期的數字。

Content: Bailey: One thing I am a little interested in, I'm not, not sure, is that, you know, we just don't have, we're not doing any marketing.
Content_zh: Bailey：有一件事我有點感興趣，也還不太確定，就是我們其實沒有做什麼行銷。

Content: We're not spending any money on marketing.
Content_zh: 我們沒有花錢做行銷。

Content: And one thing I'm just looking at is to get around that, you know, we do a lot of press presence, a lot of social media.
Content_zh: 我現在在看的其中一件事，就是為了彌補這點，我們做了很多媒體曝光、很多社群媒體。

Content: We rely upon features on the distributors, in this case with Steam and Valve.
Content_zh: 我們仰賴通路商的推薦位置，這次就是 Steam 和 Valve。

Content: But it's like what, how much does banner ads help?
Content_zh: 但問題是，橫幅廣告到底有多大幫助？

Content: And you know, those other traditional forms of marketing.
Content_zh: 還有其他那些傳統行銷形式。

Content: And it is something I'd like to look at in the future, 'cause that will be the one thing I think publishers
Content_zh: 這是我未來想研究的事，因為我覺得這會是發行商

Content: have done different than we haven't done.
Content_zh: 有做、而我們沒有做的那件事。

Content: I think, any of our fans want us to be successful commercially.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們的粉絲都希望我們在商業上成功。

Content: This whole thing is for naught if, you know, if we don't come out of it able to be more independent and then, you know,
Content_zh: 如果到最後我們不能因此變得更獨立，

Content: be able to create more creative works independently.
Content_zh: 不能更獨立地創作更多有創意的作品，那這整件事就白費了。

Content: So you know, I think we need to take that into consideration when we come up with our, our plans for Act Two.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得我們在規劃第二幕時，需要把這點納入考量。

Content: Maybe we're really lucky to have split the game in two, because then it gets some more chances.
Content_zh: 也許我們把遊戲分成兩半其實很幸運，因為這樣它就多了一些機會。

Content: And then we have more platforms coming up, so yeah, I think it will be good.
Content_zh: 接下來還會有更多平台，所以對，我覺得會不錯。

Content: Schafer: We are. Yeah, so that's just to finish the whole Red's update.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。所以這只是把整個 Red’s 的近況講完。

Content: What the team is working on now is a lot of, instead of diving into Part Two, is actually platform work.
Content_zh: 現在團隊在做的不是直接投入第二幕，而是很多平台相關工作。

Content: You get it out on more platforms, to sell it on like IOS and the other platforms.
Content_zh: 把它推出到更多平台，像 iOS 和其他平台上販售。

Content: Schafer: Sooner than we had previously thought we were going to do it.
Content_zh: 比我們原本預期要做這件事的時間更早。

Content: Schafer: The sales of the game make it look like the people who bought the game are people who played adventure games before.
Content_zh: Schafer：這款遊戲的銷售看起來，買它的人都是以前玩過冒險遊戲的人。

Content: It seems like we found those people.
Content_zh: 看來我們找到了那群人。

Content: But we haven't quite gotten yet to all the people who've never played adventure games before.
Content_zh: 但我們還沒有真正接觸到那些從來沒玩過冒險遊戲的人。

Content: Rice: The idea was while we were waiting for design and writing for Act Two stuff, that we can try and jump on some of these platform work to fill time.
Content_zh: Rice：原本的想法是，趁我們在等第二幕的設計和劇本時，先趕快做一些平台工作來填時間。

Content: And potentially even move forward some of those platform releases.
Content_zh: 甚至有可能把其中幾個平台版本的發行時間往前推。

Content: Kipnis: Hi, we have some questions for you.
Content_zh: Kipnis：嗨，我們有幾個問題想問你。

Content: Rice: We do have a pressing question for you.
Content_zh: Rice：我們確實有個緊急問題要問你。

Content: Schafer: There's a lot of work that Oliver has to get done before the iPad version can come out.
Content_zh: Schafer：iPad 版推出前，Oliver 還有很多工作得完成。

Content: But that's a version I'm waiting for.
Content_zh: 但那是我很期待的版本。

Content: Well, all of them, waiting for all of them.
Content_zh: 其實每個版本都是，我每個都在等。

Content: Hansen: From a studio standpoint the most important thing is still the platform work,
Content_zh: Hansen：以工作室的角度來看，最重要的還是平台工作，

Content: because we want to make sure that we can get the ports out as soon as possible.
Content_zh: 因為我們想確保能盡快把移植版推出去。

Content: Franzke: Right.
Content_zh: Franzke：對。

Content: Franzke: And yeah, it's been going pretty well.
Content_zh: Franzke：對，而且目前進展滿順利的。

Content: It's just, you know, it's a lot of work, just kind of looking at those platforms and seeing kind of how is the game performing on that
Content_zh: 只是工作量真的很多，要看那些平台，了解遊戲在上面的表現，

Content: and what other kind of very specific issues.
Content_zh: 還有會遇到哪些很特定的問題。

Content: Schafer: It's awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：太棒了。

Content: So I get to keep this, right?
Content_zh: 所以這個我可以留著，對吧？

Content: Franzke: Also just generally us getting the infrastructure in place to, to kind of launch games on those platforms.
Content_zh: Franzke：另外也就是把基礎架構準備好，讓我們能在那些平台上發行遊戲。

Content: Very often, you know, different storefronts require, have different requirements when it comes how you deliver the product.
Content_zh: 不同的商店常常會對你交付產品的方式有不同要求。

Content: You know, it's very different delivering something to Google Play store versus the iOS app store versus the Mac app store and things like that.
Content_zh: 把東西交給 Google Play 商店、iOS App Store、Mac App Store 之類的，做法都非常不同。

Content: So there'll a whole bunch of infrastructure things that we need to put in place.
Content_zh: 所以我們需要先建立一大堆基礎架構。

Content: And so I've been working on those a lot.
Content_zh: 我最近很多時間都在做那些。

Content: So as you can, there's still some stuff that's broken.
Content_zh: 所以你看，現在還是有一些東西壞掉。

Content: For example, some invalid textures here and there that shouldn't be there.
Content_zh: 例如這裡那裡有些不該出現的無效貼圖。

Content: I need to investigate what's going on.
Content_zh: 我得查一下到底怎麼回事。

Content: You should be able to play through the game from the beginning to end, which is huge.
Content_zh: 但應該已經可以從頭到尾玩完整款遊戲，這很重要。

Content: But as you can see, you know, the game, the game runs and I would even have sound.
Content_zh: 而且你也看得到，遊戲能跑，甚至還有聲音。

Content: [Sound from iPad] Franzke: Oh, great. All right.
Content_zh: [iPad 聲音] Franzke：喔，太好了。好。

Content: Offscreen Voice: I mean, traditionally, like port houses handle moving stuff to different platforms.
Content_zh: 畫外音：我是說，傳統上移植公司會負責把東西搬到不同平台。

Content: So like why do it internally and not just hand it off to someone?
Content_zh: 那為什麼要自己內部做，而不是直接交給別人？

Content: Franzke: Because the intrinsic goal for an external port house is different than what our intrinsic goal is.
Content_zh: Franzke：因為外部移植公司的根本目標，和我們自己的根本目標不同。

Content: For a port house, it's like, well, our contract says we have to get it done for this platform, for this amount of money.
Content_zh: 對移植公司來說，就是我們合約上寫明要用這筆錢，把它完成到這個平台上。

Content: So we will employ any hack that we can think of to make this work.
Content_zh: 所以他們會用任何想得到的 hack 讓它能跑。

Content: Like, we'll, like essentially you're throwing out any, any kind of way of like clean software development is the first thing that goes.
Content_zh: 基本上，第一個被丟掉的就是乾淨軟體開發的做法。

Content: And that's OK as a developer, if it's a product that's done and you're never going to touch it again
Content_zh: 如果你是開發商，而那是一個已經完成、之後再也不會碰的產品，那還可以。

Content: and you get the code back and you just archive it and that's it.
Content_zh: 你拿回程式碼，歸檔起來，就結束了。

Content: But if it's something that you will have to work with in the future, you don't want to do that.
Content_zh: 但如果那是你未來還得繼續處理的東西，你就不會想那樣做。

Content: Franzke: I don't know.
Content_zh: Franzke：我不知道。

Content: Criticism makes me always smile.
Content_zh: 批評總是會讓我笑。

Content: Franzke: We'll find out.
Content_zh: Franzke：到時候就知道了。

Content: Let's hope so, right?
Content_zh: 希望如此，對吧？

Content: Schafer: I made it for the hardcore fans, only the hardcore fans.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是為了鐵粉做的，只為了鐵粉。

Content: I was only thinking of the hardcore fans and I hate everyone who's not a hardcore fan.
Content_zh: 我心裡只想著鐵粉，而且我討厭所有不是鐵粉的人。

Content: Someone came up to me at PAX and was like, hey, I really like Broken Age.
Content_zh: 有人在 PAX 走過來跟我說，嘿，我真的很喜歡《Broken Age》。

Content: It seemed really short, though.
Content_zh: 只是感覺真的很短。

Content: It seemed like they cared more about getting Elijah Wood than putting money into puzzles.
Content_zh: 感覺他們比較在乎請到 Elijah Wood，而不是把錢花在謎題上。

Content: It's like, first of all, who are you saying "they"?
Content_zh: 我就想，首先，你說的「他們」是誰？

Content: That's me.
Content_zh: 那就是我。

Content: And that's not how the money worked on the project.
Content_zh: 而且這個專案的錢根本不是那樣運作的。

Content: Go and, oh why am I talking to you?
Content_zh: 去……喔，我幹嘛跟你說這些？

Content: Schafer: [High pitched voice] I was disappointed in your game.
Content_zh: Schafer：[尖聲] 我對你的遊戲很失望。

Content: I just wanted you to know.
Content_zh: 我只是想讓你知道。

Content: Schafer: You have a good point.
Content_zh: Schafer：你說得有道理。

Content: I should quit the games industry.
Content_zh: 我應該退出遊戲業。

Content: Schafer: Yay! You fans.
Content_zh: Schafer：耶！你們這些粉絲。

Content: I am excited about all the things you're asking me about.
Content_zh: 你們問我的那些東西，我都很期待。

Content: I am really, I am excited about coming on the iPad.
Content_zh: 我真的很期待它登上 iPad。

Content: I'm hoping people who have never played adventure games will give it a shot and like it and play it.
Content_zh: 我希望從來沒玩過冒險遊戲的人願意試試看，然後喜歡它、玩下去。

Content: But we didn't, you know, dumb the game down for them.
Content_zh: 但我們沒有為了他們把遊戲做笨。

Content: We just made it the way we wanted to make it.
Content_zh: 我們只是照自己想做的方式去做。

Content: Offscreen Voice: How much time do you have left to work on this?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你還剩多少時間可以做這個？

Content: 'Cause it seems like people are starting to roll back into Broken Age.
Content_zh: 因為看起來大家開始回到《Broken Age》了。

Content: Franzke: Yes. Yeah.
Content_zh: Franzke：對。對。

Content: I mean, this is the other thing that's always been happening right now.
Content_zh: 我是說，這也是現在一直在發生的另一件事。

Content: We're kind of also working on starting to work on Act Two, which is nice.
Content_zh: 我們也算是開始著手第二幕了，這很好。

Content: Schafer: Let's talk about Red's.
Content_zh: Schafer：來聊聊 Red’s 吧。

Content: Remember Red's, it's not done yet.
Content_zh: 還記得 Red’s 嗎？它還沒完成。

Content: Part One is up.
Content_zh: 第一幕已經上了。

Content: Part Two we got to do now.
Content_zh: 第二幕現在得做了。

Content: Schafer: That was crazy.
Content_zh: Schafer：那真的很瘋狂。

Content: I've never, would never have done anything like that where we shipped something and then immediately, except for like Kinect Party.
Content_zh: 我從來沒有做過那種推出某個東西後馬上又要回去做的事，除了像《Kinect Party》那樣。

Content: But I've never had to go back into a game after finishing it.
Content_zh: 但我從來沒有在完成一款遊戲後，還得再回到那款遊戲裡。

Content: Usually like, I finish the game and I immediately like look away from it and just walk off into the desert and never look at it again.
Content_zh: 通常我做完遊戲後，會立刻把視線移開，走進沙漠，再也不回頭看它。

Content: Until like ten years later or something like that.
Content_zh: 直到十年後之類的。

Content: Schafer: Well, we're so used to like shipping a game and then mentally your brain starts to heal and close off,
Content_zh: Schafer：我們太習慣推出一款遊戲後，腦袋就開始復原、封閉起來，

Content: like, it's like you would like, like you have a baby.
Content_zh: 就像生完小孩一樣。

Content: You know how it is when you guys have a baby, right?
Content_zh: 你們知道生小孩之後是什麼感覺吧？

Content: You like, and then your body starts, all right, let's pretend that never happened.
Content_zh: 然後你的身體就開始說，好吧，我們假裝那件事沒發生過。

Content: Let's just heal up, umm.
Content_zh: 先好好恢復一下，嗯。

Content: But no. It's like, there's twins.
Content_zh: 但這次不是。這就像雙胞胎。

Content: There's another one coming.
Content_zh: 還有另一個要來。

Content: Rice: Yay, twins.
Content_zh: Rice：耶，雙胞胎。

Content: Schafer: And so, normally I would just be like, OK, that's done.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以通常我會想，好，結束了。

Content: You know, what's next?
Content_zh: 接下來是什麼？

Content: But it's like, oh yeah, there's a whole other part of the meaty part of the story that we have to get into.
Content_zh: 但現在是，喔對，故事還有另一大塊真正有肉的部分得處理。

Content: Like explaining all the crazy stuff that happened in Act One.
Content_zh: 像是解釋第一幕裡發生的那些瘋狂事情。

Content: Schafer: And Lee, you're still on, you're still working on it or you're?
Content_zh: Schafer：Lee，你還在這個專案上，還在做嗎？還是？

Content: Petty: You seem surprised.
Content_zh: Petty：你看起來很驚訝。

Content: Schafer: I just know you're in demand.
Content_zh: Schafer：我只是知道你很搶手。

Content: Petty: You're in the office?
Content_zh: Petty：你在辦公室嗎？

Content: Petty: Here's all Act Two's threads I started.
Content_zh: Petty：這是我開始整理的所有第二幕線索。

Content: So these are all some of the concepts works we've been doing.
Content_zh: 這些都是我們做的一些概念工作。

Content: So you can see some of them are revisiting characters or like changes to recurring characters or scene modifications from Act One, new props.
Content_zh: 你可以看到，有些是在重訪角色，或是調整重複出現的角色、修改第一幕的場景，還有新道具。

Content: Or here's, here's like a puzzles one, you know.
Content_zh: 或是這裡，這像是謎題相關的。

Content: Curtis is now into metal working so he had all these, he needed a table in his room.
Content_zh: Curtis 現在迷上金工，所以他有這些東西，他房間裡需要一張桌子。

Content: So we were trying to figure out the positioning of the table and what they needed to do
Content_zh: 所以我們在想桌子要怎麼擺，以及他們需要做什麼，

Content: and Tim was like, yeah, that looks too useful with all those tongs on there.
Content_zh: Tim 就說，對，那上面有那麼多夾鉗，看起來太有用了。

Content: Get rid of the tongs.
Content_zh: 把夾鉗拿掉。

Content: And hey, those guys are too far apart.
Content_zh: 然後，嘿，那兩個傢伙離太遠了。

Content: They can't be in a cut scene together.
Content_zh: 他們不能一起出現在過場動畫裡。

Content: So I'm like, oh, what I'm hearing is like, yeah, that's great but now they're blocking the molds.
Content_zh: 所以我就想，喔，我聽到的是，對，那很好，但現在他們擋住模具了。

Content: So put them here and here.
Content_zh: 那就把他們放這裡和這裡。

Content: So we're kind of like, I'm trying to just work through as much as that as fast as possible without having to program anything.
Content_zh: 所以我現在就是想在不需要寫程式的情況下，盡快把這些東西都推過一遍。

Content: And I mentioned story beats so Bagel's did a lot of these early on as he kind of went through that.
Content_zh: 我剛提到故事節拍，Bagel 早期在整理時做了很多這類東西。

Content: Yeah, so a lot of this stuff is just like, you know, we're both kind of working on different aspects of the same thing, you know.
Content_zh: 對，所以很多事情就是，我們兩個都在處理同一件事的不同面向。

Content: Just kind of like print a breakdown, what the characters might be doing.
Content_zh: 有點像先印出一份拆解，看看角色可能會做什麼。

Content: I was trying to breakdown what changes to the space and puzzles would have on that.
Content_zh: 我則是在拆解空間和謎題的變化會造成什麼影響。

Content: Schafer: Right now on the project I think our animators are all out in the field, free-ranging across the company, helping out.
Content_zh: Schafer：現在這個專案上，我覺得我們的動畫師都放出去野放了，在公司各處幫忙。

Content: But our programmers are still on and we're going to meet today with anyone who's still around
Content_zh: 但我們的程式設計師還在，今天我們會和還留在這裡的人開會，

Content: to talk about the puzzles for Act Two and get that kicked off.
Content_zh: 聊第二幕的謎題，讓這件事正式開始。

Content: Schafer: Welcome, everybody.
Content_zh: Schafer：歡迎大家。

Content: Thank you for coming and still being on the Red's team, even though we shipped some of it.
Content_zh: 謝謝你們來，也謝謝你們即使我們已經推出了一部分，還留在 Red’s 團隊。

Content: Kipnis: Unlike some people.
Content_zh: Kipnis：不像某些人。

Content: Petty: Yeah, I'm like, Schafer: What is wrong with the artists?
Content_zh: Petty：對，我就像…… Schafer：美術到底怎麼了？

Content: Johnny-come-lately's.
Content_zh: 遲來的 Johnny 們。

Content: Petty: That's not everybody at all.
Content_zh: Petty：才不是所有人都這樣。

Content: Schafer: Johnny-leave-early's.
Content_zh: Schafer：早退的 Johnny 們。

Content: Kipnis: Johnny-leave-early's.
Content_zh: Kipnis：早退的 Johnny 們。

Content: Schafer: Those people who do what we've asked them to do Schafer: So we could get this design all straightened away
Content_zh: Schafer：那些只是照我們要求去做的人。Schafer：所以我們可以把這個設計全部整理好，

Content: and start implementing it, Franzke: We're done?
Content_zh: 然後開始實作。Franzke：我們做完了？

Content: Schafer: And then we're done, right?
Content_zh: Schafer：然後我們就完成了，對吧？

Content: I don't even know if there's a schedule.
Content_zh: 我甚至不知道有沒有時程。

Content: We have a schedule?
Content_zh: 我們有時程嗎？

Content: But we'll probably have a better schedule after we get this design all, actually tasked away.
Content_zh: 但等我們把這些設計都整理好，實際拆成工作後，應該會有更好的時程。

Content: Petty: Well, yeah.
Content_zh: Petty：嗯，對。

Content: I mean, I think the high level picture hasn't really changed in a long time.
Content_zh: 我是說，我覺得大方向其實很久沒有變了。

Content: You know, even before we finished Act One, I think the high level picture was still the same.
Content_zh: 甚至在我們完成第一幕之前，大方向就已經是那樣了。

Content: Yeah, it's like story beats from a long time ago.
Content_zh: 對，就像很久以前定下來的故事節拍。

Content: And he and I have talked about it on a high level.
Content_zh: 我和他也用高層次的方式聊過。

Content: So I don't think that's changed, but a lot of the very specific puzzles or cut scenes haven't been designed or written yet.
Content_zh: 所以我不覺得那有變，但很多非常具體的謎題或過場動畫還沒有設計或寫出來。

Content: And some of that has changed since Act One, you know.
Content_zh: 而有些東西自從第一幕之後也變了。

Content: So I think Tim took the feedback of a lot of people wanting a more challenging game.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得 Tim 接收了很多人想要更有挑戰性遊戲的回饋。

Content: Schafer: So how much harder are we going to make the second half of the game?
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我們要把遊戲後半段做得多難？

Content: Franzke: Much harder. Schafer: Much harder?
Content_zh: Franzke：難很多。Schafer：難很多？

Content: Schafer: Every once in a while I design something, like there's no way anyone would ever get, OK, let's put that in.
Content_zh: Schafer：我偶爾會設計出某個東西，覺得根本不可能有人解得出來，好，就放進去吧。

Content: Kipnis: You want hard?
Content_zh: Kipnis：你想要難？

Content: We'll give you hard.
Content_zh: 我們就給你難。

Content: Franzke: We're going to give you hard.
Content_zh: Franzke：我們會給你難的。

Content: Schafer: Well, it did seem trying to reconcile people complaining about the difficulty with how it felt in play-testing
Content_zh: Schafer：其實要調和大家抱怨難度，和試玩測試時的感覺，確實有點麻煩。

Content: when like people weren't getting out of the train thing.
Content_zh: 像是有人卡在火車那段出不來的時候。

Content: And like, 'cause it was a reaction to like how miserable people appeared to be when they were stuck.
Content_zh: 那比較像是因為看到玩家卡住時好像很痛苦，所以做出的反應。

Content: Kipnis: Yeah, someone, someone made a post on the forum that was really astute about how a lot of the difficulty that the old adventure games had was obfuscation.
Content_zh: Kipnis：對，論壇上有人發了一篇很敏銳的文章，講到舊冒險遊戲很多難度其實來自資訊不清。

Content: Like the fact that you had like ten verbs and you had like 20,000 items made it seem a lot harder than if it was, you know, a more limited choice or whatever.
Content_zh: 像是你有十個動詞，又有兩萬個物品，這會讓它看起來比選項比較有限時難很多。

Content: Franzke: And no Internet.
Content_zh: Franzke：而且沒有網路。

Content: Kipnis: Yeah, and no Internet, exactly.
Content_zh: Kipnis：對，而且沒有網路，沒錯。

Content: Franzke: No, seriously. Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: Franzke：不，真的。Schafer：對。

Content: and no real play testing.
Content_zh: 也沒有真正的試玩測試。

Content: Some play-testing, but not like we did.
Content_zh: 有一些試玩測試，但不像我們這樣。

Content: We've kind of gone for like slicker, slicker inventory.
Content_zh: 我們算是做了更順、更精簡的物品欄。

Content: And we did that over the course of those adventure games, too.
Content_zh: 那些冒險遊戲一路下來，我們其實也有在這樣做。

Content: Like Throttle had like a very small inventory and of course people said that was too easy and short.
Content_zh: 像《Full Throttle》的物品欄就很小，當然大家也說它太簡單、太短。

Content: There you go.
Content_zh: 就是這樣。

Content: Schafer: I mean, would we want to add another row to the inventory because of that?
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，我們會因此想在物品欄再加一排嗎？

Content: Would we want to have a double stack inventory?
Content_zh: 我們會想做雙層物品欄嗎？

Content: Like Vella shrinks down and then Shay can carry her around?
Content_zh: 比如 Vella 縮小，然後 Shay 可以把她帶著走？

Content: And you can open her up and get her inventory. Whoa.
Content_zh: 然後你可以打開她，拿到她的物品欄。哇。

Content: I'm trying to just, just general good design stuff is watching out for things where you find a solution before you find a problem.
Content_zh: 我只是想做一般好的設計，也就是注意不要在找到問題前就先找到解法。

Content: And you know, I mean I was trying to find those in Act One, too.
Content_zh: 你知道，我在第一幕也有試著找出這些東西。

Content: But some things you just never, are not clear.
Content_zh: 但有些事情就是永遠不會那麼清楚。

Content: Well, let me fly through this and then we can just highlight some things to come back to later.
Content_zh: 好，讓我快速過一遍，然後我們可以標出之後要回頭看的東西。

Content: Well, there's some like challenges I'm trying to achieve with the second act, like the interaction between Shay and Vella.
Content_zh: 第二幕有一些我想達成的挑戰，像是 Shay 和 Vella 之間的互動。

Content: Maybe not in direct interaction, but more relevance of the switching back and forth, making that more relevant.
Content_zh: 也許不是直接互動，而是讓來回切換更有意義。

Content: Even if it's just information that passes back and forth in your brain.
Content_zh: 就算只是資訊在你腦中來回傳遞也好。

Content: And I'm really thinking about how to wrap up the character arcs in the end, 'cause mostly with adventure games you have a story
Content_zh: 我也真的在思考最後要怎麼收束角色弧線，因為冒險遊戲通常會有故事，

Content: but you tend to think about the progress of the story as being the puzzles.
Content_zh: 但你往往會把故事的進展想成謎題的進展。

Content: And I solve a whole bunch of puzzles and then they win, do you know what I mean?
Content_zh: 我解了一大堆謎題，然後他們就贏了，你懂我的意思嗎？

Content: Whereas if you're writing a movie you'd be thinking about the total ups and downs these characters are going through.
Content_zh: 可是如果你在寫電影，就會思考這些角色經歷的整體起伏。

Content: And so I'm focusing a lot more on that, 'cause I really want it to be satisfying, how the characters end up in the end.
Content_zh: 所以我更專注在這點上，因為我真的希望角色最後的結局能讓人滿足。

Content: Not just they achieved all their small goals and add it to one big goal, but they actually had a moment of you know, some real lows and highs
Content_zh: 不只是他們達成了所有小目標，累積成一個大目標，而是他們真的有那種真正的低潮和高潮，

Content: and that the ending actually meant something to the characters.
Content_zh: 而且結局對角色來說真的有意義。

Content: Hopefully.
Content_zh: 希望如此。

Content: But it's easy to get distracted by the mechanics of solving puzzles and think that the story is kind of resolving.
Content_zh: 但很容易被解謎機制分心，覺得故事好像就這樣解決了。

Content: And the "A" plot does resolve in adventure games.
Content_zh: 冒險遊戲裡的 A 劇情確實會解決。

Content: Like your action plot resolves because the character achieves their goals, but the "B" plot's like the emotional plot often, it's kind of left.
Content_zh: 也就是行動線會解決，因為角色達成了目標，但 B 劇情，也就是情感線，常常就被放著。

Content: Where it's summed up by the two characters kissing at the end or something like that.
Content_zh: 最後可能用兩個角色接吻之類的方式帶過。

Content: But to have actually sum up in a meaningful way for the characters is something I have to do.
Content_zh: 但要讓它真正以對角色有意義的方式收束，是我必須做到的事。

Content: Schafer: The game?
Content_zh: Schafer：遊戲嗎？

Content: Schafer: May? No.
Content_zh: Schafer：五月？不。

Content: I really am trying everything to make sure the game comes out this year.
Content_zh: 我真的在想盡一切辦法，確保遊戲今年推出。

Content: And then people always go what?
Content_zh: 然後大家總是會說，什麼？

Content: And then I say, the first act took two years.
Content_zh: 然後我就說，第一幕花了兩年。

Content: And so if the second act can take less than one year, it's a miracle.
Content_zh: 所以如果第二幕能在不到一年內完成，那就是奇蹟。

Content: And that's what we're doing.
Content_zh: 而那就是我們現在在做的事。

Content: Rice: If the directive is we want to ship by the end of the year, I think we've talked a lot about like things that would need to happen
Content_zh: Rice：如果指示是我們想在年底前推出，我覺得我們已經聊了很多需要發生的事，

Content: to make that doable, to like where if we timeboxed a scheduled to December,
Content_zh: 才能讓這件事可行。也就是如果我們把時程框到十二月，

Content: we'd still have to do some stuff to pull it in a little bit.
Content_zh: 我們還是得做些事情，才能把進度拉回來一點。

Content: I think that's the conversation that we need to have, I guess.
Content_zh: 我想這就是我們需要進行的對話。

Content: 'Cause if not, like it's going to be very easy to just keep pushing stuff back like we did on Act One.
Content_zh: 因為不然的話，就會很容易像第一幕那樣，不斷把東西往後推。

Content: Schafer: Well, the two big reasons, two of the big reasons are, or three, there's like backer expectations because they,
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，兩個主要原因，其中兩個，或三個，其中一個是贊助者的期待，因為他們，

Content: a lot of people thought it was going to come out like two months after Act One.
Content_zh: 很多人以為它會在第一幕之後大概兩個月推出。

Content: So that message thing that we kind of have to get out to people.
Content_zh: 所以我們必須把那個訊息傳達給大家。

Content: Like a couple people I've talked to, it's like I hope it's coming out this year, so there's like that.
Content_zh: 像我聊過的幾個人都說，希望它今年會出，所以有這點。

Content: There is financial, just that Justin has been working with December in all his financial planning, you know.
Content_zh: 還有財務因素，Justin 在所有財務規劃裡一直都是用十二月在算。

Content: So that's a pretty hard thing.
Content_zh: 所以這是相當硬的限制。

Content: Rice: Yeah, both for getting profit in and for spending on the project.
Content_zh: Rice：對，無論是收入進帳，還是專案支出都是。

Content: Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。

Content: And for me, just the possibility of actually being up for awards because we shipped the first half this year.
Content_zh: 對我來說，還有一點是我們今年推出了前半部，所以實際上有機會角逐獎項。

Content: So we're either going to be not eligible for 2014 awards or really coming out in January or March of 2015,
Content_zh: 不然我們要不是沒有 2014 年獎項資格，就是真的拖到 2015 年一月或三月才推出，

Content: when everyone will forget about it by the time the '15 awards come out.
Content_zh: 等到 2015 年獎項出來時，大家早就忘了它。

Content: So there's that.
Content_zh: 所以也有這點。

Content: Rice: I think those are all the right reasons and all the reasons that I feel like everyone I've talked to about this wants to really make December happen,
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得這些都是正確的理由，而且我和大家聊到這件事時，大家也都真的想讓十二月成真。

Content: but I think that it's, it's just going in knowing that that would mean like, doing whatever it takes to make that happen.
Content_zh: 但我覺得重點是要先知道，那代表我們得不惜一切代價做到。

Content: And sacrificing in some ways.
Content_zh: 也得在某些方面做出犧牲。

Content: Petty: It's possible. It's possible.
Content_zh: Petty：有可能。有可能。

Content: You know, most video game productions are a series of bottlenecks.
Content_zh: 你知道，大多數電玩製作都是一連串瓶頸。

Content: And trying to get everything unblocked with minimal bottlenecks is what's going to make or break December.
Content_zh: 盡量用最少的瓶頸把所有事情疏通，會決定十二月能不能成。

Content: Although the other problem with December is there's a lot of unknowns as it comes to puzzle playability and testing.
Content_zh: 不過十二月的另一個問題是，謎題的可玩性和測試有很多未知數。

Content: So we can take certain steps, like we can try and work through earlier versions or script early versions and test them,
Content_zh: 所以我們可以採取某些步驟，像是先做早期版本或早期腳本，然後拿去測試，

Content: but you know, ultimately that's the fun factor and that's a little hard to know how that's going to hit.
Content_zh: 但最終那還是樂趣問題，而那有點難知道實際效果會怎樣。

Content: And when you don't leave yourself a lot of iteration time, it may not be enough and you don't really know.
Content_zh: 如果你沒有留下很多反覆調整的時間，可能就不夠，而你其實不會知道。

Content: Schafer: I think we can do December.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得我們可以做到十二月。

Content: To me it's like an infinity far away.
Content_zh: 對我來說，那還像是無限遙遠。

Content: I always think we can do anything.
Content_zh: 我總覺得我們什麼都做得到。

Content: That's why my games are sometimes late.
Content_zh: 所以我的遊戲有時候才會延期。

Content: Schafer: It's really just the best game that we can make and we feel like we can actually make without crashing the company into the ground.
Content_zh: Schafer：這真的只是我們能做出的最佳遊戲，而且我們覺得能在不把公司拖垮的情況下做出來。

Content: And they also just want to finish it because it's, while it's fresh in people's minds.
Content_zh: 大家也只是想趁它還在玩家記憶裡時把它完成。

Content: And I do want to get the team back and there's a pressure to keep them on other projects because they have these publisher milestones.
Content_zh: 我也確實想把團隊找回來，但也有壓力要讓他們繼續待在其他專案上，因為那些專案有發行商里程碑。

Content: And publisher milestones are always scarier than internal milestones, because there's no outside force threatening to
Content_zh: 發行商里程碑總是比內部里程碑可怕，因為有外部力量威脅你，

Content: not pay you if you don't meet the milestones.
Content_zh: 如果你沒達成里程碑，他們就不付錢。

Content: So we have to be that threatening outside force.
Content_zh: 所以我們得變成那個有威脅性的外部力量。

Content: Can you make some phone calls from outside the building with a funny voice?
Content_zh: 你可以用怪聲音從大樓外面打幾通電話嗎？

Content: Hansen: I can.
Content_zh: Hansen：可以。

Content: Schafer: And be like, we won't pay you guys if you don't meet your milestones on this project.
Content_zh: Schafer：然後說，如果你們這個專案沒達成里程碑，我們就不付錢。

Content: Hansen: I'm pretty sure they're going to figure out that it's me.
Content_zh: Hansen：我很確定他們會發現是我。

Content: Schafer: Well, that's the other big factor, right.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，那也是另一個大因素，對吧。

Content: Is we went to this smaller team, which I think from a budget standpoint was a good idea, 'cause you were writing.
Content_zh: 就是我們變成比較小的團隊，從預算角度看我覺得是好主意，因為你在寫劇本。

Content: Bagel and I could do some early concept work and stuff.
Content_zh: Bagel 和我可以做一些早期概念工作之類的。

Content: If we were working from a no, this is the target deadline, that is not a good way to work.
Content_zh: 但如果我們一開始就是，不，這就是目標期限，那就不是好做法。

Content: If it's a, this is our target budget and so we're lowering the berm while we figure stuff out.
Content_zh: 如果是，這是我們的目標預算，所以我們在摸索時先降低消耗，那可以。

Content: But if we were like, no, this is an unmovable time here in December, then we should have, I mean this is in hindsight,
Content_zh: 但如果我們是說，不，十二月這個時間點不能動，那我們應該要，我是說事後看來，

Content: but we probably should have tried to get more of the team and start full production like right after AF or something.
Content_zh: 我們大概應該試著找回更多團隊，在 AF 之後之類的時間就開始全面製作。

Content: Hansen: Yeah, but we couldn't have.
Content_zh: Hansen：對，但我們當時也做不到。

Content: There wasn't enough design to execute on at that point.
Content_zh: 那時候沒有足夠的設計可以執行。

Content: Petty: Perhaps. But I mean, I'm just, you know, that's, I think it's just one of the other factors,
Content_zh: Petty：也許吧。但我是說，我只是覺得，這也是其他因素之一。

Content: I think, is that it felt good to have a small team for a while, while we sort of like could digest some of these things
Content_zh: 我覺得有一陣子維持小團隊感覺很好，讓我們可以消化這些事情，

Content: and Oliver was working on a platform.
Content_zh: 而 Oliver 也在做平台。

Content: But it did sort of put this maybe end date a little bit in jeopardy.
Content_zh: 但這確實讓這個可能的結束日期有點危險。

Content: Team Member: That's very true.
Content_zh: 團隊成員：這倒是真的。

Content: Schafer: So maybe Oliver's stuff will make so much money we can have more time to make Act Two.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以也許 Oliver 那邊會賺超多錢，讓我們有更多時間做第二幕。

Content: Rice: It sounds like we're not going to have more time, though, right?
Content_zh: Rice：不過聽起來我們不會有更多時間，對吧？

Content: It sounds like we could have more money to throw at the problem and put...
Content_zh: 聽起來我們可能會有更多錢可以砸向問題，然後把……

Content: Schafer: Oh yeah. Maybe that will make more, we'll buy some more people.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔對。也許那會賺更多，我們就多買幾個人。

Content: Franzke: Yeah. It works pretty well.
Content_zh: Franzke：對。運作得滿好的。

Content: I'm overall, like pretty happy with how it turned out.
Content_zh: 整體來說，我對成果滿滿意的。

Content: The load times aren't terrible.
Content_zh: 載入時間不算糟。

Content: You know, we were able to get a lot of visual fidelity out of the game.
Content_zh: 我們也讓遊戲保留了不少視覺品質。

Content: You know, essentially making it look as good as on PC, which I'm pretty happy about.
Content_zh: 基本上讓它看起來跟 PC 上差不多好，這點我滿開心的。

Content: So if I go on the app store.
Content_zh: 所以如果我打開 App Store。

Content: What is cool, is like obviously if you go to games, it's right there, Broken Age.
Content_zh: 很酷的是，如果你去遊戲分類，它就在那裡，《Broken Age》。

Content: It's also in Best New Games, which is nice.
Content_zh: 它也在最佳新遊戲裡，這很不錯。

Content: And that seems to be trending pretty well also in the charts.
Content_zh: 而且排行榜上看起來表現也不錯。

Content: So I would say overall, so far it's been a really successful launch.
Content_zh: 所以整體來說，目前為止這次發行很成功。

Content: Schafer: Last night I spent unknown hours playing through Broken Age and I'm so glad I did but I didn't know I was going to end unfinished.
Content_zh: Schafer：昨晚我花了不知道幾個小時玩《Broken Age》，我很慶幸有玩，但我不知道最後會停在未完待續。

Content: Uh oh.
Content_zh: 糟糕。

Content: We don't really call it Part One on the app store.
Content_zh: 我們在 App Store 上其實沒有叫它第一部。

Content: I should say Part, should I say, does this guy say Part Two coming soon.
Content_zh: 我應該說第……我該說嗎？這個人有說第二幕即將推出嗎？

Content: Should I say "soon"?
Content_zh: 我該說「即將」嗎？

Content: If children love this as much as Day of the Tentacle or Psychonauts, then please mark me as fuddy-duddy and ignore me.
Content_zh: 如果孩子們像愛《Day of the Tentacle》或《Psychonauts》一樣愛這款遊戲，那就請把我當成老古板，無視我吧。

Content: OK, where do I do that?
Content_zh: 好，我要去哪裡標記？

Content: It was like no game I've ever played.
Content_zh: 這跟我玩過的任何遊戲都不一樣。

Content: It doesn't glitch or crash.
Content_zh: 它不會出 bug，也不會當掉。

Content: The story is great and I can't wait for Act Two.
Content_zh: 故事很棒，我等不及第二幕了。

Content: Two stars.
Content_zh: 兩顆星。

Content: Not as advertised.
Content_zh: 和宣傳不符。

Content: This game says ages 9 through 11 and that's about all I think would be fun for.
Content_zh: 這款遊戲標示適合 9 到 11 歲，我覺得也只有那年紀會覺得好玩。

Content: Oh, and the fake train wreck?
Content_zh: 喔，還有那個假的火車事故？

Content: Why are we doing this over and over again?
Content_zh: 為什麼我們要一直重複做這件事？

Content: The kid actually says he'd rather jump off a cliff than do it again or something like that.
Content_zh: 那孩子其實說他寧願跳下懸崖，也不想再做一次之類的。

Content: That's how I feel as well.
Content_zh: 我也有同感。

Content: Please refund.
Content_zh: 請退款。

Content: There's no way this game is worth the price.
Content_zh: 這遊戲絕對不值這個價錢。

Content: I think he' stuck on the train.
Content_zh: 我覺得他卡在火車那裡了。

Content: So maybe that wasn't too easy after all.
Content_zh: 所以也許它終究沒有太簡單。

Content: Anyway. Overall it's really good.
Content_zh: 總之，整體真的很不錯。

Content: On the iPad.
Content_zh: 在 iPad 上。

Content: Schafer: Very happy with the iPad launch.
Content_zh: Schafer：我對 iPad 發行非常滿意。

Content: I'm good.
Content_zh: 我沒問題了。

Content: Franzke: I think we will know in a few days, kind of from some of our third-party tools that send us information about app sales.
Content_zh: Franzke：我想幾天後，我們會從一些第三方工具知道，它們會提供 App 銷售資訊。

Content: But yeah, fingers crossed.
Content_zh: 不過對，祈禱順利吧。

Content: Bailey: So moving on, Broken Age did decent sales on iPhone.
Content_zh: Bailey：那接著說，《Broken Age》在 iPhone 上銷售還不錯。

Content: Stamos: iPad.
Content_zh: Stamos：iPad。

Content: Bailey: iPad, sorry.
Content_zh: Bailey：iPad，抱歉。

Content: Decent enough that we're looking into doing iPhone and potentially android tablets with it.
Content_zh: 表現夠好，所以我們正在研究做 iPhone 版，也可能做 Android 平板版。

Content: So that was good.
Content_zh: 所以那很好。

Content: It's meeting forecasts.
Content_zh: 它符合預測。

Content: It's not like a runaway hit, but it's, you know, it's good.
Content_zh: 不是那種爆紅作品，但還不錯。

Content: It's decent cash flow.
Content_zh: 現金流還可以。

Content: So the numbers right now are about 100,000 for, you know, four days.
Content_zh: 所以目前數字大概是四天十萬美元左右。

Content: So expect for the actual feature week, while I'd say editor's choice, it'll probably be around 150,000
Content_zh: 所以實際推薦週，也就是編輯精選期間，我估計大概會到十五萬美元左右，

Content: and then it'll probably go down about one-fourth of that for the next week, while it's as a previous editor's choice.
Content_zh: 然後下一週變成前編輯精選時，可能會降到大概四分之一。

Content: So yeah, decent, decent numbers.
Content_zh: 所以對，數字還不錯。

Content: Bailey: So it was received well.
Content_zh: Bailey：所以反應不錯。

Content: It looks like it's, it's, it's doing what our expectations were sales-wise.
Content_zh: 看起來銷售方面符合我們的預期。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Expectations?
Content_zh: 畫外音：預期？

Content: Bailey: Yeah, I mean you can expect, you know, about 100 to $150,000 is what I expect out of a week
Content_zh: Bailey：對，我是說，一款以內容為主的 iOS 遊戲，一週大概可以期待 10 萬到 15 萬美元。

Content: for an iOS game that's a content-based game. Yeah.
Content_zh: 對，以內容為主的 iOS 遊戲。

Content: Schafer: I think he was telling me they performed to his expectations.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想他的意思是，它們的表現符合他的預期。

Content: They met his sales forecast, but of course forecasts are always pretty conservative.
Content_zh: 它們達到了他的銷售預測，不過預測當然通常都很保守。

Content: So he's always secretly hoping that his sales forecasts are wrong.
Content_zh: 所以他心裡總是偷偷希望自己的銷售預測是錯的。

Content: Bailey: My expectation, just to be clear, though, they're more about, my expectations are more reflective of the current market
Content_zh: Bailey：不過先說清楚，我的預期比較是，反映目前市場狀況。

Content: on like iTunes for premium based games.
Content_zh: 像 iTunes 上那些高價遊戲。

Content: And like paid content based games.
Content_zh: 以及付費、內容導向的遊戲。

Content: So it's less of a reflection about the game and more a reflection about the market.
Content_zh: 所以它比較不是反映這款遊戲本身，而是反映市場。

Content: Schafer: We were at number four, now we're at number ten.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們本來在第四名，現在是第十名。

Content: We might not be number ten anymore, but it was fun to be number four for a long time.
Content_zh: 也許我們現在已經不是第十名了，但能在第四名待很久還滿有趣的。

Content: Well, we finally got pushed off ten.
Content_zh: 好，我們終於被擠出前十名了。

Content: Let's see how far down we are. Twenty-two.
Content_zh: 來看看掉到多低。二十二名。

Content: This looks dirty.
Content_zh: 這看起來很髒。

Content: Beaten by "Frozen Karaoke".
Content_zh: 被《Frozen Karaoke》打敗了。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, I mean, I'm happy with the performance on iPad, but I don't think it made a lot of extra money that's going to allow us
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我是說，我對 iPad 上的表現很滿意，但我不覺得它賺了很多額外的錢，足以讓我們

Content: to like hire another programmer and throw him at the team.
Content_zh: 再雇一個程式設計師丟到團隊裡。

Content: So I don't think it's going to change the schedule, unfortunately.
Content_zh: 所以很遺憾，我不覺得它會改變時程。

Content: We can always hope.
Content_zh: 我們總是可以抱著希望。

Content: I keep promising that I will stop hoping for those kind of things, but it's hard not to think about like, what if it was a big hit,
Content_zh: 我一直承諾自己不要再對這種事抱希望，但很難不去想，如果它爆紅了，

Content: then we could totally hire a programmer to help finish it.
Content_zh: 那我們就完全可以雇一個程式設計師來幫忙完成它。

Content: But I don't know.
Content_zh: 但我不知道。

Content: But I'm just happy it made enough that we can stay in business.
Content_zh: 我只是很高興它賺得夠讓我們繼續營運。

Content: Bailey: It's just there's no magic bullet, basically.
Content_zh: Bailey：基本上就是沒有什麼萬靈丹。

Content: So you know, it's, everything's a grind, especially in the game industry.
Content_zh: 所以你知道，一切都很辛苦，尤其是在遊戲業。

Content: Like, you know, you have to have an amazing press and get the feature spots and get huge VO and have amazing polish and then do OK.
Content_zh: 像是你得有超棒的媒體聲量、拿到推薦位置、請到超大咖 VO、打磨得很漂亮，然後結果只是還可以。

Content: But if you didn't have any of that stuff, you'd do horrible.
Content_zh: 但如果那些東西你都沒有，就會慘得要命。

Content: So the, yeah. The hit.
Content_zh: 所以，對。爆紅這件事。

Content: The hit has been, has been elusive, for sure.
Content_zh: 爆紅這件事真的一直很難捉摸。

Content: [Music] Schafer: What did you guys think of it?
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：你們覺得怎麼樣？

Content: Offscreen Voice: What's that notepad that says, "the end" on it, right there.
Content_zh: 畫外音：那本寫著「結局」的筆記本是什麼？就在那裡。

Content: Schafer: Oh. This is how things are going to end.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔。這是事情最後會怎麼結束。

Content: This is the guy who guessed that Shay's ship was Mog Chothra.
Content_zh: 這是那個猜到 Shay 的船就是 Mog Chothra 的人。

Content: I'm super happy that no one seemed to have guessed the big spoiler at the end of it.
Content_zh: 我超開心，好像沒有人猜到最後那個大劇透。

Content: I really thought that was going to get out.
Content_zh: 我真的以為那會被爆出來。

Content: And actually long ago on the forum one guy guessed it.
Content_zh: 其實很久以前論壇上有個人猜到了。

Content: And no one followed up on it.
Content_zh: 但沒有人接著討論。

Content: Like no one, he just said his little thing, hey, what if this is the case?
Content_zh: 就像沒人理他，他只是說了自己的小想法，嘿，如果是這樣呢？

Content: No one answered him.
Content_zh: 沒有人回他。

Content: I was like, oh man.
Content_zh: 我就想，天啊。

Content: Schafer: By the way, since I was the guy who guessed that, by the way, here's how I figured out how it, how this, why I'm so smart.
Content_zh: Schafer：順帶一提，既然我是猜到那件事的人，順帶一提，這是我怎麼推理出來的，這就是我為什麼這麼聰明。

Content: And then here's how what I think is going to happen in Act Two.
Content_zh: 然後這是我覺得第二幕會發生的事。

Content: Offscreen Voice: He's done it for you.
Content_zh: 畫外音：他都替你做完了。

Content: Schafer: Yeah. What if I get a bunch of ideas from him.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。萬一我從他那裡拿到一堆點子怎麼辦？

Content: Might be bad.
Content_zh: 可能不太妙。

Content: People send me stuff as if I have time to read anything.
Content_zh: 大家寄東西給我，好像我有時間看任何東西一樣。
